location.office_time_required: 5 days a week in San Francisco

secondary_archetype: Head of Workplace / Real Estate & Facilities

seniority.level: Senior Director / Head-of (likely Director or VP)
level_signals: 8-10 years of experience in Facilities, Workplace Experience, Security and/or Real Estate leadership, including leading teams of various sizes
Owns global real estate and workplace strategy
Partners directly with senior leadership and executive team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8-10 years of experience in Facilities, Workplace Experience, Security and/or Real Estate leadership, including leading teams of various sizes

Proven success at companies in the high tech space that evolved into first spaces, multiple spaces and then to regional spaces, with a track record of being scrappy, creative, and resourceful in scaling operations efficiently
Demonstrated ability to build rapport and credibility with senior leadership and site leaders, driving alignment on long-term real estate strategy as well as day-to-day operations
Passion for creating people-centered, inclusive environments, through design and experiences
Belief in the power of design and brand to build meaningful workplace experiences and engagement with a strong aesthetic sensibility and an eye for how physical space reinforces brand identity
Experience establishing and managing physical security programs, including day-to-day security operations and Executive Protection
Proven ability to build, develop, and empower a team by fostering a culture of ownership, accountability, and local impact
Strong project management and vendor negotiation skills
Experience building relationships and collaborating with the Executive Team, People, IT, Legal, Finance and other business leaders
Strong change management skills, especially in growth phases

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: LEED Accreditation – U.S. Green Building Council
Project Management Certification (e.g. PMP) – a plus
OSHA or local safety compliance training

scope.surface: Global workplace strategy and operations across offices in San Francisco, New York, Austin, London, and Berlin
scope.business_model: B2B/B2D SaaS — developer infrastructure and agentic platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Facilities cost management and budget adherence
Lease negotiation outcomes and real estate cost efficiency
Security program effectiveness and incident rates
core_responsibilities: Own global real estate and workplace strategy
Lead site selection, lease negotiations, and build-outs
Create spaces designed for collaboration, focus, wellness, and belonging
Be the guardian of Vercel's brand and aesthetic in the physical world
Own day-to-day operations across all locations
Build and run physical security programs including Executive Protection
Develop health, safety, and compliance programs
Own the workplace budget and manage vendor ecosystem

compensation.equity_form: equity-based compensation mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($220K-$265K base) plus equity
Clear scope of offices named (SF, NY, Austin, London, Berlin)
Well-defined responsibilities across strategy, experience, operations, and budget
Vercel encourages applicants who don't check every box
Role has broad executive visibility and ownership
red_flags: Strictly onsite 5 days a week in San Francisco — eliminates all non-SF candidates or those preferring any flexibility
Compensation outside San Francisco may be adjusted downward, though role is SF-only
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
8-10 year range is framed as a specific band which may exclude very senior candidates above that range

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, team ownership, and executive partnership signals)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
ambiguities: The JD says 'Compensation outside of San Francisco may be adjusted based on employee location,' but the role is explicitly stated as San Francisco 5 days a week — this clause appears to be boilerplate and may not apply to this specific role.
The years range '8-10 years' is unusually narrow for a Head-of role; it is unclear whether candidates with 11+ years would be screened out or whether this is aspirational framing.
